search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae chronic kidney disease guideline UAE primary care
site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD evaluation chronicity abnormal kidney function monitoring referral
candidate_sources_rejected: single eGFR value treated as established CKD
automatic CKD stage, cause, progression, medicine change, repeat interval, or nephrology referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Chronicity and context are required before CKD classification.
All interpretation and action remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with an abnormal kidney-function result or established CKD context; acute kidney injury, pregnancy, children, dialysis, transplant, obstruction, and unstable systemic illness require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact CKD detection and chronicity section supports reviewing prior and current kidney-function results, timing, trends, albuminuria, imaging, and clinical context without diagnosing CKD from one abnormal result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact cause-evaluation section supports clinician-entered medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, electrolyte, and imaging context only where assessed or reviewed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact monitoring section supports recording measured observations, kidney laboratory trends, albuminuria, and existing reports without interpreting progression automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports clinician-entered plan, follow-up, safety-net, and specialist-status documentation only after qualified review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact analyte, value, reference range, baseline, volume status, acute illness, pregnancy, cause, stage, medicine, repeat interval, referral threshold, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
